questions.en: Read the statements about "A Letter to God". Statement 1: Lencho's house was in the middle of a populous town. Statement 2: Lencho was a good farmer. Choose the correct option.
options: Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true

questions.en: "I hope it passes quickly." What is the 'it' referred to in "A Letter to God"?
What was Lencho's immediate response when he received the letter that he believed was from God?
options: He began shouting at the postmaster.
He was not surprised, but he became angry.

questions.en: In Mandela's account of the inauguration, he was accompanied by his daughter named:
When were two national anthems played in "Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom"?
options: At the opening of World Peace Day
On South Africa's New Year's Day

questions.en: Read the statements about Mandela. Statement 1: On inauguration day, he was overwhelmed with a sense of history. Statement 2: He learned that courage is triumph over fear, not the absence of fear. Choose the correct option.
During September to March, Coorg is described as having a season of:
The theory of Arab origin of the people of Coorg is based on:
Read the statements about "Coorg". Statement 1: Visitors are fascinated by its adventurous activities. Statement 2: The people of Coorg are hostile and cowardly. Choose the correct option.
options: Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true

questions.en: In "Tea from Assam", what does the expression "Pranjol buried his nose in his detective book" mean?
options: He became deeply engrossed in reading.
He tried to smell the book.

questions.en: Read the statements about the tea legend. Statement 1: Bodhidharma was a lazy monk who loved to sleep. Statement 2: The legend of Bodhidharma originated in China. Choose the correct option.
options: Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true

questions.en: The second-flush or sprouting period is important because it:
Read the statements about "Madam Rides the Bus". Statement 1: Valli loved standing at the front door. Statement 2: Her playmates were of her age. Choose the correct option.
options: Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true

questions.en: In "Madam Rides the Bus", what was Valli planning and replanning carefully?
In the line "He stalks in his vivid stripes", the word "vivid" means:
In the poem "Amanda!", Amanda is asked not to:
In "Animals", Walt Whitman writes that animals do not discuss their duty to:
In "The Ball Poem", the boy senses his first responsibility in a world of:
Why does the speaker in "The Ball Poem" say that he would not intrude on the boy?
options: Another ball cannot replace the boy's loss.
The boy will not listen to him.
He does not want to disturb a game.
He does not know how to speak.

questions.en: In "The Ball Poem", choose the opposite of "desperate".
According to "The Tale of Custard the Dragon", which colour pair is correct?

Read the statements about "Footprints without Feet". Statement 1: Griffin chose a bad time to be in London. Statement 2: It was a bad time because all shops in London were closed. Choose the correct option.
options: Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true

questions.en: What experiment had Griffin carried out in "Footprints without Feet"?
options: How to develop an artificial brain

questions.en: In "Footprints without Feet", choose the word nearest in meaning to "curious" in "a curious episode occurred".
Read the statements about "A Question of Trust". Statement 1: Horace Danby loved to read books. Statement 2: Book lovers can be cheated easily by beautiful women. Choose the correct option.
options: Statement 1 is true and Statement 2 is false
Statement 1 is false and Statement 2 is true

questions.en: Horace Danby had been sent to prison fifteen years earlier because:
options: he tried to rob an old man
he pushed a traveller from a bus

questions.en: Horace gets angry when anyone discusses "honour among _____."
Read the sentences: (1) We have to be careful and alert. (2) Is that so, but why? Identify their sentence types.
options: Sentence 1 is imperative and Sentence 2 is exclamatory.
Sentence 1 is assertive and Sentence 2 is interrogative.

questions.en: In the question "Do you have any money?", the word "any" functions as a:
In the sentence "I signed my name where father told me to do," the clause "where father told me to do" is an adverb clause of:
Identify the grammatically correct sentence.
options: This hotel offer fooding and lodging.
This hotel offer food and lodging.
This hotel offers food and lodge.
This hotel offers food and lodging.

questions.en: Choose the correct equivalent of "I want to be free."
Complete the sentence: "The train will stop _____ many stations."
In the sentence "We should speak the truth," the modal "should" implies:
Choose the correct passive form of "He was writing a letter."
options: A letter was being written by him.
A letter was wrote by him.
He was being writing a letter.

questions.en: Choose the correct active form of "Let the door be closed."
Complete the sentence meaningfully: "Raju used to earn by _____."
In her imaginary world in "Amanda!", Amanda plays the role of:
In "Animals", the speaker wonders where the animals got the _____ from.
In the extract from "Madam Rides the Bus", Valli "stifled" her desire to spend her saved fare. The word "stifled" means:
In the village fair episode, why was it difficult for Valli to preserve her saved bus fare?
options: She had no friends at the fair.
She was forced to leave early.
She did not carry any money.
She had to resist riding the merry-go-round though she could pay for it.

questions.en: In the passage on displaced children with disabilities, their experiences vary widely according to individual:
A survey reports school attendance rates of 7.5% for self-care difficulty, 21% for cognitive difficulty, 23% for depression/confusion and 27% for walking difficulty. Choose the ascending order.

Read the statements from the disability-and-education passage. Statement 1: Access to education is difficult for children with disabilities. Statement 2: Children with disabilities in low and middle income countries suffer more. Choose the correct option.
In the 2011 survey described in the passage, children with difficulty in seeing were most likely to have access to:
